title: Cruel Angels Thesis
wikitext:
[]: Cruel Angel's Thesis             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun May 03, 2009 4:24 am Do the lyrics mean anything. They always seemed to be a mixture of being about destiny and things about the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun May 03, 2009 10:43 am I don't feel like going through line by line. Think "coming of age" for the most part.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun May 03, 2009 11:34 am I've heard there's a version sung by the seiyuu for Misato, Asuka, and Rei, were there is supposedly some vague correspondence between the lines they sing respectively, and their characters' POV towards Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun May 03, 2009 12:13 pm If you read the lyrics while listening to that version, you'll see that's the case. Also, the second half of the song fits in with 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun May 03, 2009 2:34 pm Wasn't Cruel Angel's Thesis a song before Evangelion? Then most of the lyrics wouldn't correspond precisely to things in the show (unless Gainax was working unusually hard). The lyrics are probably more like clueing you in on some abstract images, (poetic language) that don't necessarily connect to anything easily in reality (like letting cruel angels into--oh wait, that means something!).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun May 03, 2009 2:35 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: Wasn't Cruel Angel's Thesis a song before Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun May 03, 2009 2:40 pm I probably should have checked wikipedia before posting...

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Mon May 04, 2009 2:37 am Oedipusfoot wrote: I don't feel like going through line by line. Think "coming of age" for the most part.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Mon May 04, 2009 2:06 pm Not nerdy enough? You mean you want me to make stuff up? I was looking over three different translations to the Eva OP today, and each one meant something different. Wanna chose the version?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed May 06, 2009 12:12 pm Reichu wrote: Oedipusfoot wrote: Wasn't Cruel Angel's Thesis a song before Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed May 06, 2009 3:13 pm It should also be noted that I checked the Refrain of Evangelion liner notes and didn't see any such thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu May 07, 2009 3:17 am Oedipusfoot wrote: I probably should have checked wikipedia before posting...

[]:             Quote Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Fri May 08, 2009 2:16 am Ornette wrote: It should also be noted that I checked the Refrain of Evangelion liner notes and didn't see any such thing.

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Fri May 08, 2009 8:59 pm Anime opening songs rarely make any sense or have anything to do with the story at all. That might be the case.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sat May 09, 2009 11:06 pm ah the wonders of wikipedia. and yes it is possible its just random. but this is evangelion, so if anno had much to do with it, its a big complex meaningful poet pile of lyrics, that can be disected with years of continually rewatching the show. I hope its not.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun May 10, 2009 12:48 pm Anno didn't write it, right? It was some "Neko" person. And if this makes sense to you, MOON TIME The shadow of the tower you're looking up is getting longer People are gathering on the moonlight night once more Moon Time Moon Time Moon Time Opening the forgotten sky, Moon Time, getting down Burning the lost-days-city's lights above the people, now The hard sticking wind is blowing In the distance, someone is crying again Moon Time Moon Time Moon Time then you have a good chance of finally uncovering the mystery of the i-muh-jry brought on by the poetics of Neko's artistic sense. Actually, Hirasawa makes sense most of the time, even though you are confronted with just atmospheres and images. I imagine Anno saying to Neko, "Go forth, and make pop, so that we may live on this earth in peace."
summary:
[]: Cruel Angel's Thesis             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun May 03, 2009 4:24 am Do the lyrics mean anything. They always seemed to be a mixture of being about destiny and things about the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun May 03, 2009 10:43 am I don't feel like going through line by line. Think "coming of age" for the most part.

Article: Cruel Angels Thesis
, right? It was some "Neko" person. And if this makes sense to you, MOON TIME The shadow of the tower you're looking up is getting longer People are gathering on the moonlight night once more Moon Time Moon Time Moon Time Opening the forgotten sky, Moon Time, getting down Burning the lost-days-city's lights above the people, now The hard sticking wind is blowing In the distance, someone is crying again Moon Time Moon Time Moon Time then you have a good chance of finally uncovering the mystery of the i-muh-jry brought on by the poetics of Neko's artistic sense. Actually, Hirasawa makes sense most of the time, even though you are confronted with just atmospheres and images. I imagine Anno saying to Neko, "Go forth, and make pop, so that we may live on this earth in peace."
